t:morgan-findings|c:019c5e9e-28fd-7ea7-b.idea: AI compliance copilot for SMB HR teams

teaser.oneLiner: AI compliance copilot for SMB HR teams: a focused, governed launch path for the first 90 days.
teaser.positioning: This launch positions the offer for teams that need fast execution with strict governance and clear outcomes.
teaser.icpSnapshot: Best initial customers are founder-led B2B teams with urgent workflow pain and willingness to buy a measurable fix.
teaser.monetizationAngle: Start with a clear paid pilot and convert to recurring subscriptions once onboarding and first value are proven.
teaser.strategicDifferentiator: The wedge is deterministic execution plus auditable receipts, reducing risk while keeping time-to-value short.
teaser.ctaUnlockValue: Get the complete 90-day operating blueprint with execution sequencing, risks, and first five actions.

blueprint.title: Startup 74770d4a Blueprint v1
blueprint.executiveThesis: Focus on one high-friction customer segment, deliver a narrow paid outcome quickly, then scale only after repeatable proof of value is established.
blueprint.offerArchitecture: Package the core service as a tiered offer: a paid diagnostic sprint, a guided implementation phase, and an ongoing optimization layer with clear ownership boundaries.
blueprint.monetizationModel: Use upfront implementation fees plus recurring subscriptions tied to execution cadence, with explicit upgrade paths as customers move from pilot to production usage.
blueprint.distributionStrategy: Begin with founder-led outbound to niche communities and partner channels, then formalize referral loops once early wins and testimonials are documented.

ninetyDayPlan.weeks1to3: Finalize positioning, tighten offer scope, and onboard 3 pilot customers with strict success criteria and weekly decision checkpoints.
ninetyDayPlan.weeks4to8: Instrument delivery metrics, remove operational bottlenecks, and convert pilot learnings into reusable playbooks and onboarding assets.
ninetyDayPlan.weeks9to12: Scale controlled acquisition, harden retention loops, and formalize expansion motions based on proven unit economics and customer outcomes.

blueprint.riskMitigation: Mitigate execution risk by enforcing scope control, preserving weekly governance reviews, and failing closed whenever receipt, policy, or delivery quality checks are incomplete.
firstFiveActions: Define one narrow ICP and reject adjacent segments for this quarter.
Ship a paid pilot offer with explicit success and failure criteria.
Set weekly governance checkpoints with decision logs and receipts.
Capture customer evidence and convert it into repeatable playbooks.
Lock expansion until retention and delivery consistency are stable.

t:morgan-findings|c:019c632c-26bd-7008-b.idea: I want to create a not-for-profit business called "The Orchard".  It will be a large piece of land with various fruits and vegetables. Customers can purchase $5 and $10 buckets and/or $20 wagons. They walk around the orchard (or rent a cart to ride) and dill their containers with as much produce as they can fit in them. When they are ready to leave, we bag them nicely.
